overview: Despite its name, this deck does not utilize the Energy mechanic. Instead, it is a fast, resilient aggressive strategy that leverages haste, lifelink, and combat tricks to apply relentless pressure. The game plan revolves around deploying efficient threats early, protecting them with targeted removal, and closing games before opponents can stabilize.

keep_criteria: Hands with 2-3 lands and at least one 1- or 2-mana creature
Hands containing early removal or combat tricks
Hands with Ragavan or Ocelot Pride alongside a cheap spell
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or threats

examples.reason: Provides turn-one pressure, early removal, and a solid mana base.
Too many lands and lacks early-game presence or interaction to apply pressure.

early_game: Use cheap removal to clear blockers
Establish early board presence to force opponent reactions
mid_game: Deploy Ragavan and Ocelot Pride to accelerate damage
Utilize combat tricks and lifelink to stabilize against board wipes
Sequence removal to protect key threats
late_game: Close out with resilient, high-impact threats like Phlage
Leverage Guide of Souls for recursion and value
Maintain pressure to prevent opponent from setting up win conditions

key_cards.name: Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury
key_cards.notes: High toughness and lifelink make it resilient; attacks for significant damage while stabilizing against removal.

key_cards.name: Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer
key_cards.notes: Provides turn-one haste, card draw, and relentless combat damage.

key_cards.name: Ocelot Pride
key_cards.notes: Flying and haste bypass blockers; synergizes with combat tricks to close games.

key_cards.name: Guide of Souls
key_cards.notes: Returns creatures from graveyard to hand, enabling repeated pressure and protecting against board wipes.

sequencing_tips: Hold up removal until you see what the opponent plays
Attack with Ragavan before casting spells to maximize card advantage
Use lifelink triggers to stabilize against sweepers
Sequence combat tricks to maximize damage while maintaining blockers if needed

matchups.advice: Apply maximum early pressure and use evasion to bypass counterspells. Prioritize protecting your win conditions.
Use removal efficiently to clear the path. Hold up interaction for their key threats and race with resilient creatures.
Race with haste threats and use lifelink to stabilize. Sequence removal to protect your board while applying pressure.
